# N-Link

> N-Link is a local, provider-agnostic memory, RAG and governance brain for AI
> agents. It runs on the user's machine as an MCP server and gives any
> MCP-capable AI persistent long-term memory, relevance-gated recall, a failure
> log that prevents repeated mistakes, per-action approval governance, an
> independent quality gate, and cross-AI task handoff. Memories live in a local
> SQLite database and never leave the user's machine.

Key facts:
- Works with any MCP client: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, others.
- Model-agnostic: the brain serves its own operating protocol to every AI that
  connects, so a session started with one model (Claude, Gemini, GPT) can be
  continued by another from the exact task state the previous one left.
- 28 brain tools over MCP: session bootstrap, task analysis, deployment
  planning, approval tiers, quality council, memory and learning, operations.
- Privacy: all memory is local (SQLite); the default mode sends nothing
  anywhere. The optional premium engine mode sends only the analyzed task text
  to a user-configured, authenticated endpoint.
- Quality: work is scored against a fixed 10-criterion gate; critical work is
  judged by independent AI auditors (never self-scored) and can be audited
  cross-model.
- Governance: side-effectful actions are risk-tiered; critical, irreversible
  actions (money, contracts, deletion) are locked to explicit owner approval.
- Open-core: the shipped core is readable source with an honest basic engine;
  the proprietary N-Language engine (the technology that understands the true
  intent behind words) is served remotely and fails closed.
- Status: private beta. Waitlist on the landing page.

## Pages
- [Landing page](https://tnkfreelance.github.io/nlink-site/): product overview, how it works, FAQ (EN/JA).
